items.headline: Knicks on Brink of NBA Finals After Dominant Game 3 Victory Over Cavaliers
items.summary: The New York Knicks are just one win away from their first NBA Finals appearance since 1999, taking a commanding 3-0 lead in the Eastern Conference Finals with a 121-108 triumph over the Cleveland Cavaliers in Game 3. Jalen Brunson led the charge with 30 points.

items.headline: Thunder Hold 2-1 Lead Over Spurs in Western Conference Finals Ahead of Crucial Game 4
items.summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder maintain a 2-1 series lead against the San Antonio Spurs in the Western Conference Finals. Game 4 is set for today, Sunday, May 24th, promising another thrilling matchup in the tight series.

items.headline: Knicks Dominate Cavaliers in Game 3
items.summary: New York extended their playoff win streak to 10 games, putting them on the verge of a sweep against Cleveland.

items.headline: Jalen Brunson Leads Knicks' Attack
items.statContext: Brunson's game-high performance powered the Knicks to their Game 3 victory over the Cavaliers.

items.headline: Mikal Bridges Delivers Key Scoring for NYK
items.statContext: Bridges contributed significantly to the Knicks' offense in their win against the Cavaliers.

items.headline: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's All-Around Effort
items.statContext: SGA's strong showing helped the Thunder secure their Game 3 win over the Spurs on May 22nd, maintaining their series lead.

items.headline: Victor Wembanyama's Strong Outing
items.statContext: Wembanyama put up solid numbers in the Spurs' Game 3 loss to the Thunder on May 22nd.

items.headline: Evan Mobley Leads Cavs' Scoring Effort
items.statContext: Mobley was the top scorer for the Cavaliers in their Game 3 loss to the Knicks.

items.headline: Spurs' Key Players Clear Injury Report for Game 4
items.summary: De'Aaron Fox (ankle) and Dylan Harper (adductor) are both off the injury report and expected to play in Game 4 of the Western Conference Finals against the Oklahoma City Thunder.

items.headline: Thunder Battling Injuries as Playoffs Intensify
items.summary: OKC's Jalen Williams (left hamstring tightness) is expected to return today, May 24th, while Ajay Mitchell (right calf strain) is slated to return by May 26th.

items.headline: Upcoming Showdowns: Crucial Playoff Games on the Horizon
items.summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder will host the San Antonio Spurs for Game 4 of the Western Conference Finals tonight at 8:00 PM EDT. The New York Knicks and Cleveland Cavaliers will resume their Eastern Conference Finals series with Game 4 on Monday, May 25th, at 8:10 PM ET.
